steps.title: Read the word in context
steps.content: Mr. Ryder has already decided to propose; the only thing left is to "determine the time when he would ask her to be his wife." He then picks a specific occasion (the ball). So "determine" here describes the act of settling on or picking a particular moment.

steps.title: Test a substitute
steps.content: Plug each option back into "determine the time" and see which preserves the meaning of selecting a specific moment to propose. Only one captures the idea of making a deliberate selection.

steps.title: Eliminate by misfit
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Choose': "choose the time when he would ask her" fits perfectly — he is selecting a specific moment. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Influence': he is not affecting or swaying the time; one cannot sway a moment in time. Wrong sense of the word. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Demonstrate': he is not displaying or proving the time to anyone; nothing is being shown. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Measure': he is not quantifying duration; he is picking an occasion. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
